supply_chain.dependencies: Pharmacy benefit management services outsourced to CVS Caremark for claims processing, pharmacy network contracting, rebate processing and mail and specialty pharmacy fulfillment services
supply_chain.risks: Dependence on third-party vendors for information technology services and pharmacy benefit management; vendor failures could adversely affect operations

regulatory_risks: Federal or state legislative or regulatory changes to Medicaid, Medicare, or Marketplace programs including funding changes, block grants, per capita caps, work requirements, non-renewal of Marketplace subsidies, provider taxes, or amendments of the Affordable Care Act
Budget pressures on state governments and states' efforts to reduce rates or limit rate increases

Changes in interpretation or implementation of federal or state medical cost expenditure floors, administrative cost and profit ceilings, premium stabilization programs, profit-sharing arrangements, and risk adjustment provisions
HIPAA and HITECH Act compliance requirements for privacy and security of protected health information
Federal and state fraud and abuse laws including anti-kickback statutes, False Claims Act, and civil monetary penalties
State licensing and solvency requirements including minimum statutory capital and surplus requirements
Regulatory restrictions on dividends and distributions by health plan subsidiaries
Risk adjustment data validation (RADV) audits by CMS with potential for extrapolation of audit results and payment adjustments
Medicare Star Ratings requirements with potential for contract non-renewal if ratings fall below 3.0 for three consecutive years
State and federal privacy laws including California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA), and Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA)
Healthcare reform proposals that could significantly reduce federal or state spending on Medicaid and Medicare programs
Potential elimination or reduction of advanced premium tax credits and cost sharing reductions for Marketplace
Changes to Medicare Advantage risk adjustment model and phase-in of new methodology through 2026
Transition requirements for Medicare-Medicaid Plans (MMPs) to end no later than December 2025
Compliance with No Surprises Act and independent dispute resolution requirements for out-of-network providers
State-specific minimum medical loss ratio requirements and quality performance measures
Federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) adjustments and retroactive premium rate changes by states
